user_query:
I’m preparing a short briefing for our storage lab meeting. Please coordinate a small local workflow to run the included benchmark simulator, extract inventory data, rank results, and draft an email to the group.

Requirements and inputs:
- From the project root, run the provided Python script at tools/simulate_bench.py. It deterministically simulates 12 storage benchmark runs and will:
  - Write a JSON Lines file at generated/raw_metrics.jsonl containing only the successful runs with fields: device, fs, qd, throughput_mb_s, latency_ms.
  - Print per-run status lines to stdout and print any failures to stderr. It exits with a non-zero status if any runs fail.
- The included inventory file input/inventory.html lists device-to-model mappings and related metadata in a single HTML table.

Deliverables (create these exact files):
1) output/inventory.json
   - Extract the device inventory from input/inventory.html into structured JSON. Represent each row as an object with exactly these fields: node, device, model, capacity_tb.
2) output/metrics_enriched.csv
   - Parse generated/raw_metrics.jsonl and enrich each successful record by joining the model from output/inventory.json on the device field.
   - Include columns in this order: device, model, fs, qd, throughput_mb_s, latency_ms.
   - Sort all rows by throughput_mb_s (descending), breaking ties by latency_ms (ascending).
3) output/top3.json
   - Save the top 3 ranked configurations from output/metrics_enriched.csv as a JSON array of objects with the same fields as in the CSV.
4) output/failures.txt
   - Analyze the command’s exit code and stderr to identify which runs failed and why. For each failed run, include one line with: device, fs, qd, and the root cause message extracted from the error text. (Only stderr contains failure details; the JSONL intentionally omits failed runs.)
5) output/email_draft.txt
   - Draft a concise email to storage-lab@dept.edu with:
     - A subject line stating the best-performing configuration (device, model, fs, qd) by throughput and including its throughput and latency.
     - A brief body summarizing the top 3 configurations (with device, model, fs, qd, throughput_mb_s, latency_ms), the count of failed runs, and a bullet-style or paragraph list of failure causes routed from output/failures.txt.
     - A short recommendation for next steps (e.g., re-run suggestions for the failed cases).

Constraints:
- Use only the provided files and standard tools (no Internet needed).
- The simulator’s stderr and exit status must be analyzed to populate output/failures.txt; do not infer failures from the JSONL alone.
- Ensure all specified output paths are created under the workspace.

This will help me quickly brief the team with the top configurations and known issues. Thanks! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
